name: Sortable File Explorer

releases.author: Ninjalope
releases.description:
- Replace manual frontmatter parsing with MetadataCache.getFileCache()
- Use window-scoped timer functions (window.setTimeout, window.setInterval,
  etc.) instead of bare globals
- Replace custom trash logic with FileManager.trashFile()
- move static indicator styles to CSS, keep dynamic positioning inline
- Improve drag-and-drop hierarchy targeting and preview behavior.
- Fix incorrect root drop zone activation in item gaps,
- Make folder move hints appear consistently across valid target areas
- Stabilize the custom drag image on first drag.
- fixed multi-select options: "move to folder" and "new folder with"
releases.name: Plugin submission changes, bug fixes, better dnd detection and previews

releases.author: Ninjalope
releases.description:
-Explorer now uses full sidebar height
-Removed duplicate "New Base" option from context menu
releases.name: Bug Fixes

releases.author: Ninjalope
releases.description:
- Removed settings h2.
- scoped and prefixed all css classes so there's no inference with Obsidian's native classes.
releases.name: Changes for Obsidian Plugin Submission

releases.author: Ninjalope
releases.description:
Fixed: Folders were collapsing when files were renamed. 
Fixed: Title line of a file was repeatedly highlighting after a file was created. This cased the typing to overwrite previously typed characters.
releases.name: Fixed bugs for folders collapsing and title line highlights

releases.author: Ninjalope
releases.description:
### Overview
Alternative file explorer with custom sorting and improved drag & drop. This plugin replaces Obsidian’s default file explorer with a powerful, interactive sorter while keeping a familiar feel.

### Features
Drag & drop reordering for files and folders (custom sort order)
Multi-select (Shift for ranges; Cmd/Ctrl for toggling)
Inline rename (F2) and context menus for common actions
Batch operations (move/duplicate/delete multiple items)
Precise drop indicators and smart folder drop zones
Keyboard navigation (arrows, Enter, Home/End, Cmd/Ctrl+A)
External import: drag files from Finder/Explorer into your vault
Visual active-file highlight and selection outlines
### Requirements
Obsidian 1.9.0 or newer
Desktop: Full feature set
Mobile: Loads and reflects your custom order; drag & drop and some desktop actions aren’t available on touch
### Installation
Community Plugins (recommended): Settings → Community plugins → Browse → search “Sortable File explorer” → Install → Enable.
### Manual:
Download main.js, manifest.json, and styles.css from this release (or the zip).
Create folder: .obsidian/plugins/sortable-file-explorer
Place the files inside that folder.
Reload Obsidian or toggle the plugin.
Known limitations
Touch devices don’t support desktop-style drag & drop or right‑click context menus.
Some third‑party context menu extensions may vary in compatibility.
### Support
Created by Ninjalope. If you find this helpful, consider supporting: https://ko-fi.com/ninjalope
